events.title: Events
events.chooseLocation: Choose your location and sign up. Only one submission is needed.
events.taiwanTitle: Upcoming Book Club in Taipei, Taiwan
events.taiwanDesc:
In Barbara Demick's Daughters of the Bamboo Grove, twin sisters Fangfang and Shuangjie become the thread that reveals child trafficking under the one-child era, bureaucratic corruption, and the gray industry of international adoption.

Through investigative storytelling, Demick shows how policy can tear apart families and identities. She not only reconstructs the fate of trafficked children, but also follows two families through emotionally complex reunions shaped by civilizational and language barriers.

The book also confronts the moral dilemmas of international adoption and global inequality, helping readers see the systemic historical forces and consequences beyond any single personal story.
events.nlDesc:
The Netherlands is only about 1.15 times the size of Taiwan. In some ways we feel alike, yet in many ways we are strikingly different.

Through three lenses - transition, society, and environmental sustainability - this book explores both the bright and shadowed sides of Dutch life.
It questions workplace norms, gender diversity in practice, and the realities faced by working women.
It also dives into a topic rarely covered by mainstream media: the deeply rooted sports culture in Dutch society.

After finishing this book, you will also learn:
Why has cannabis long remained in a legal gray zone?
Can the so-called "Dutch disease" be cured?
Does the Netherlands have its own north-south divide?
And what histories lie behind the Red Light District?
events.onlineDesc: Seeking a regular space to refine your spoken English? Enhance your fluency through the joy of reading. We know that the greatest challenge isn't getting it "right," but simply having the courage to speak out. To guarantee a high standard of discussion, we strictly limit each group to 15 participants. Feel free to choose between "Peer-only" slots or "Expat-participant" slots. ．Recommended Level: B2 - C1 ．Highlights: Boutique group sizes throughout.
events.detoxDesc:
Welcome to Bookdigest’s cozy little Digital Detox!

For a few hours, we all unplug.
No screens, no scrolling, just books, shared food, and brilliant strangers who might become your next favourite conversation.
We wanted a space to slow down, where sharing a book feels like sharing a piece of yourself, and being present is the only thing that matters.
Come unplug with us, your cosy little digital detox awaits.

digitalDetox.title: Digital Detox
digitalDetox.description: A gentle reset from constant notifications. Put your phone away for a while and join us in slow, mindful reading.
digitalDetox.step1: Pick a time window to unplug (start with 25 minutes).
digitalDetox.step2: Place your phone out of reach; set it to Do Not Disturb.
digitalDetox.step3: Read a book or reflect in a notebook. That's it.
digitalDetox.tip: Tip: Start small, be consistent. We'll check in weekly at the book club.

joinus.title: How to become a Book Digest Host?
joinus.subtitle: Choose your location and sign up to join our book club community. Only one submission is needed.
joinus.hostIntro: As a Book Digest Host, you will:
joinus.hostPoint1: Suggest or help select the monthly book/theme
joinus.hostPoint2: Help secure a cozy offline venue (we'll guide you!)
joinus.hostPoint3: Facilitate the group vibe so everyone feels welcome to join the chat
joinus.hostPoint5: Most importantly: create a space where discussions feel easy, meaningful, and fun!
joinus.hostKitTitle: We'll provide you with a full Host Starter Kit, including book selection tips, discussion prompts, icebreaker ideas, and more. So even if it's your first time hosting, you're in good hands!
joinus.whyJoinDesc:
You'll become part of the Book Digest family, with access to our upcoming brand collaborations, community projects, and publisher partnerships.

You'll get early access to new books, join monthly host calls with our founder and fellow hosts, and grow alongside a creative, thoughtful reading crew.

It's a chance to build your own space, make friends across industries, and grow your facilitation skills (with our full support!).
joinus.whereDesc:
We currently host gatherings in Taipei, the Netherlands, and the UK.

But wherever you are (even if we already have a group in that city) you're welcome to propose a new chapter!

We'll support you with event tools, templates, registration systems, and help promote your events through our network.

Let's build your local Book Digest together!
joinus.uncertainDesc:
It's okay to try before you commit!

You're welcome to join an upcoming book club session first, get a feel for the vibe, and then decide if hosting is right for you.
joinus.applyDesc: Fill out our short Host Application Form, tell us your ideas and where you're based, and we'll be in touch via email when we're ready to welcome you into the Book Digest crew!

books.title: Books
books.notesNotice: Want to be notified when updates are available? Join our book club!
books.question1: What impressed you the most about this book?
books.question2: How would you apply the concepts from this book in your life?
books.question3: Are there any viewpoints you disagree with?

about.storyPara1: It all began in a café in Taipei’s East District, where a group of young people returning from abroad found one another through a shared love of films and books. As we went along, we kept experimenting and refining how we choose our reads and run our discussions, so the gatherings became more than book chat. They became a space to learn together, listen well, and share life. That’s how Bookdigest came to be.
about.storyPara2: Each month, we take turns picking the book, ranging from novels and psychology to world history and personal finance. Sometimes it’s a book you’d happily pick up yourself; other times it gently nudges you beyond your comfort zone. Seeing the world through someone else’s choice keeps every month fresh and full of surprises.
about.storyPara3: We believe reading isn’t a one way intake, but a process of circling back, challenging ideas together, and letting them settle over time. Only when you chew on what you have read does it truly become your own. We meet in a different café each month. If you love a good book, a meaningful conversation, and a great cup of coffee, come join us!
about.reason1Desc:
Each month, we will pick the book, and trust us, it's never the same.
From fiction to psychology, world history to personal finance, you'll find yourself reading stuff you'd never pick on your own.
It's like a mini adventure outside your comfort zone, and honestly, those surprises are often the best part!
about.reason2Desc:
We come from all sorts of backgrounds — engineers, designers, teachers, finance folks, you name it.
And when we read something topic-specific, we sometimes invite someone with real-life experience to join the conversation.
That's what makes our book club both deep and fun at the same time!
about.reason3Desc:
Think of it more like catching up with friends than a formal discussion.
No need to prep a presentation, just bring the book you read (and your lovely self)!
Everyone shares their favorite chapters or adds something extra the book didn't cover. Come hang out and chat with us!

terms.contact: For any questions about these Terms, please contact us at

privacy.contact: If you have any questions about this privacy policy, please contact us at

notFound.title: Page Not Found
notFound.description: Sorry, the page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.

signupFlow.thanksBody:
To make sure everyone has enough time and space for meaningful discussion, we limit the number of participants per session. Starting in 2026, we will collect a small NT$ 100 registration fee to help cover venue, website, and admin costs, and to encourage attendance.

This helps us keep the quality of each gathering consistent — so that every one of you who comes can enjoy a rich and worthwhile book club experience. Thank you for your understanding and support!
signupFlow.richartPrefix: Or tap to open the Richart app with the account prefilled:
signupFlow.remitPrompt: After you transfer, please fill this in so we can confirm your payment:
signupFlow.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again later.
signupFlow.fullBody: This session has reached its capacity. Please watch our next event announcement.
signupFlow.closedBody: Registration is currently closed for this time slot.
signupFlow.successBody: Thanks for signing up! We'll send a confirmation email one week before the event with the venue and important notes. Please keep an eye on your inbox.
signupFlow.cancelBody: Because spots are limited and we need to arrange the venue and admin in advance, we generally do not offer refunds after confirmation. If you have an unexpected situation and cannot attend, please contact us as soon as possible. We will help you transfer your spot — you may transfer your seat to a friend or family member and let us know their contact information at least three days before the event.
signupFlow.contact: Questions or ideas? DM our page or email us:
signupFlow.finalNote: We usually send the venue details about a week before the event — please keep an eye out. Looking forward to seeing you there!

error.title: Something went wrong
